entry.title: 🇵🇱 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Thu 21 May 2026 in Kraków, PL, kindly hosted (including lunch & refreshments) by GE HealthCare, thank you very much! (See below for specific arrival instructions).

⚠️Note that capacity for this CodeJam is 30 people, so please ensure you use the RSVP links on this page accordingly.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

Specific arrival instructions: After entering the building, please sign in with your name at the journal. Then turn right and proceed to the 2nd floor, where reception will issue your guest badge."

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: SAP Developer News for April 16th, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS

UX Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on 11 June 

  • Event page https://events.sap.com/us-ux-innovation-day-siliconvalley-2026/en_us/home.html  

SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2605 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-pre-upgrade-option-for-release-2605/ba-p/14347147  

XML Annotations: The Essentials Before Adapting Documentation Samples to Your Project 

  • Mio’s blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/xml-annotations-the-essentials-before-adapting-documentation-samples-to/ba-p/14369241 
  • OData Deep Dive mission https://developers.sap.com/mission.odata-deep-dive.html  
  • OData Deep Dive rewrite blog post https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2026/02/02/odata-deep-dive-rewrite-in-the-open/  

Edge Integration Cell APIs and Cloud Integration AI-assisted error resolution 

  • Edge Integration Cell APIs: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/local-odata-api-access-for-edge-integration-cell?locale=en-US  
  • AI-assisted error resolution: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/analyze-message-processing-errors-with-ai-f27f95d7b90b4f13aea22b399418b702?locale=en-US  

DYK #12 AI Launchpad Leaderboard 

  • The Model Library topic in the SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/docs/ai-launchpad/sap-ai-launchpad/model-library  

CHAPTER TITLES

00:00 Intro 

00:07 UX Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on 11 June 

01:00 SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2605 

02:00 XML Annotations: The Essentials Before Adapting Documentation Samples to Your Project 

03:16 Edge Integration Cell APIs and Cloud Integration AI-assisted error resolution 

04:25 DYK #12 AI Launchpad Leaderboard 

TRANSCRIPT

[INTRO]
This is the SAP Developer News for April the 16th, 2026.

[DJ]
If you're in the Silicon Valley area in June and are interested in the intersection between AI and UX, then there's an event just for you. It's on the 11th of June at the SAP AppHaus in Palo Alto, and it's an in-person event only. It's for developers, architects, and technical consultants, and it's all about the AI functionality being built into the SAP Fiori elements framework. There's AI for business users and AI for application developers. The event page has an agenda for the day, which is currently tentative, but it gives us a good idea of the format and content. There's also a link on there for registration. Anyway, check it out. Link in the description.

[Mamikee]
We have an important update for SAP BTP ABAP environment users. We're offering a free pre-upgrade option for release 2605, giving customers and partners the chance to test their existing applications ahead of the standard upgrade, which is planned for May 16th and 17th. Now some of the key milestones have already passed. Planned new features were announced on April April 13th, and pre-upgraded systems are scheduled for upgrade on April 17th, which means teams already participating should now be focused on regression testing and validating their custom-built apps. Now, looking ahead, Hot Fix Collection 2 is planned for April 30th, and any issues found during testing should be reported by May 1st using component BC-CP-ABA. Now, the goal is simple. help make the upgrade a no-event and avoid day one surprises. You can read more in the link below.

[DJ]
Fiori elements based UIs are great, but sometimes it's hard for us to grok how they can be controlled and configured, which is mostly through annotations, which are organized in vocabularies, expressed in metadata, which is usually in the form of XML. So any help is welcome. And in that context, we wanted to point you to a great blog post by SAP community member Mio Yasutake, who takes time to explain these topics in great detail, how things fit together based upon samples and documentation for UI5 and in the context of CAP and RAP-based services. There's lots of detail and it's well worth a read. Thanks Mio! Also if you want more detail to complement this then we have the OData deep dive mission over on the tutorial navigator and in there there are specific tutorials on metadata vocabularies and annotations ready to go as well. Happy learning!

[Antonio]
Hola, SAP Developers! Two quick updates for Edge Integration Cell and Cloud Integration users. First, the local OData APIs for Edge Integration Cell just got better. You can now authenticate using Client ID and Client Secret. More secure, easy to automate. Now, a quick reminder that there are many APIs in Edge Integration Cell that you can use. Monitoring APIs, for example, to track message processing. and management APIs for message stores, data stores, JMS brokers, and variables. These are perfect for building custom dashboards or for automating operations or integrating with your existing monitoring tools. Now, in Cloud Integration, we have AI-assisted error resolution. When your integration flow fails, you can now use AI to analyze the error, get root cause suggestions, and even get remediation steps. So it's like having an integration buddy next to you. which can save time debugging and gets you back on track faster. So check the links in the description for full documentation on both features. ¡Hasta luego!

[Nora]
Did you know that you can compare foundation models right in AI Launchpad? So just jump over to Generative AI Hub in your AI Launchpad and open the model library. And then here you can click on Leaderboard. And now you see all the models that we have available here. and over here you can see the different scores so the HELM score for example tells you how well the model performed in a multiple choice exam across a variety of topics the chatbot arena score tells you how well the model performed in a head-to -head against different AI models and then the AIRBench refusal rate and the AIRBench discrimination bias refusal rate tells you how well the model is handling harmful or biased requests and then all the way over here you have the output and input token cost and the context window size. Now this really helped me to find the right model for my use case depending on cost and context window size and performance overall. I honestly usually don't go to the leaderboard, I head directly to the chart because here you can very easily compare the models.

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer News for April 16th, 2026
entry.content: Thanks @MioYasutake , and you're welcome. I plan to extend the series beyond what's already there, I just have some CodeJams and talks to prepare and give in the near future, first 👍

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Fri 18 Sep 2026 in Weinheim, DE, kindly hosted by Syntax, thank you very much! Note that it takes place on the day before the SAP Inside Track Weinheim event which is on Sat 19 Sep 2026.

(For more info on this double-event, see the blog post Two days of SAP community in Weinheim: CodeJam and Inside Track, 18-19 September 2026).

⚠️ Registration for this CodeJam must be done on the SAP CodeJam Weinheim - Hands-on with CAP CDS page which has been specially set up for this event.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

entry.title: SAP Developer News for April 23rd, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS 

Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/migration-tool-for-your-sap-build-apps-frontend-web-applications/ba-p/14368805  
  • SAP Help page https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-apps-path-forward#migrating-from-the-sap-build-apps-standalone-offering-to-sap-build 

SAP Open Source Webinar: Olympic Sailing on Eclipse Technology – From Closed Source to Open Source 

  • Event info https://events.sap.com/olympic-sailing-on-eclipse-technology-from-closed-source-to-open/en_us/home.html  

SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #3 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/customer-amp-partner-roundtable-for-sap-btp-platform-automation-3-april/ba-p/14374109  

DYK #13 Override default auth while using xsuaa (CAP) 

  • Capire: Authentication in Production – Enforced by Default https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/authentication#enforced-by-default  

CHAPTER TITLES 

00:00 Intro 
00:07 Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications 
01:31 SAP Open Source Webinar: Olympic Sailing on Eclipse Technology – From Closed Source to Open Source 
02:24 SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #3 
03:24 DYK #13 Override default auth while using xsuaa (CAP) 

TRANSCRIPT

[Intro]
This is the SAP Developer News for April the 23rd, 2026.

[Shrini]
Hello, SAP community. By now, you may have seen the announcement about SAP Build Apps deprecation as of March 2026. Understandably, the first question on everyone's mind is, what happens to the apps I am already building? The team has been listening closely to the feedback from the community. To make this transition as seamless as possible, SAP has rolled out a migration tool tool for your SAP Build Apps projects. This tool will transform your existing front-end web applications into a fully structured React or SAP UI5 project. Once transformed, you can easily continue enhancing them within your SAP Build environment using Business Application Studio. If you are ready to migrate, you can reach out to SAP by opening a support ticket with the mtar file of your project, specify whether the app should be based on React or SAPUI5 and authorize the transformation. Detailed instructions on creating this ticket are available on this help page. The link can be found in the description. This tool was built based on your feedback. Let us know how this works for you. Finally, a quick reminder, you will continue to have full access to SAP BuildApps for the entire duration of your current contract.

[Mamikee]
Sailing Analytics is now open source, and if you want to learn more about the journey behind it, there's an upcoming webinar you don't want to miss. On May 20th, 2026 at 3pm CEST, join the technical webinar on Eclipse Azimuth Sailing Analytics, a real-time regatta tracking and analysis tool that has been used at more than 1,000 sailing events, including the Olympic Games. In the session, product owner Axel will walk through SAP's journey to open source the solution, including licensing strategy, internal processing, collaboration with the Eclipse Foundation, and community building efforts. Now, if you're interested in open source, real-world analytics solutions, or how projects like this evolve beyond SAP, this is definitely one to check out. You can learn more and register in the link below.

[DJ]
Are you interested in the Terraform and CLI tools for managing your BTP resources? Sharing opinions and experiences on this topic with others? Then the BTP Platform Automation Customer and Partner Roundtable series is for you. And the third instance of the series is just around the corner. It's on Tuesday, the 12th of May at As usual, there will be some info on latest updates, a round of Q&A, some demos and more. There's a blog
post with further details, a link that you can use to register. It's free, by the way. And also in that blog post, there's a link to content from the previous two instances. See you there.

[Shrini]
Did you know you can override the default authentication behavior for CAP service endpoints using a simple feature flag? By default, when you deploy a CAP service to Cloud Foundry with a production -grade authentication strategy, all service endpoints are protected. This happens because CAP implicitly adds the @requires authenticated user annotation to every service. But what if you want to make some endpoints publicly accessible, especially during testing or for specific use cases? You can disable this default behavior by adding the flag RestrictAllServicesToFalse to your package.json. In this batch request service, setting the RestrictAllServices flag to false makes all service endpoints publicly accessible by default, meaning no authentication is required. However, the createBatch action is explicitly protected using the @RequiresAuthenticatedUser annotation, ensuring that only authenticated users can invoke it.

entry.title: 🇵🇱 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Fri 22 May 2026 in Wrocław, PL, kindly hosted by Capgemini, thank you very much! Note that it takes place on the day before the SAP Inside Track Wrocław event which is on Sat 23 May 2026. 

⚠️Registration for this CodeJam must be done on the SAP Essentials: SAP CodeJam - Hands-on with CAP CDS page which has been specially set up for this event. UPDATE 11 May 2026 - Please note that the registration has now reached maximum capacity.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Thu 24 Sep 2026 in Paris, FR, kindly hosted by VINCI Energies Systèmes d'Information, thank you very much! Note that it takes place on the day before the planned SAP Inside Track Paris event on Fri 25 Sep 2026. 

⚠️Note that capacity for this CodeJam is 30 people, so please ensure you use the RSVP links on this page accordingly.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge!
entry.content:
SAP Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge

Welcome to the Developer Challenge for this month of June 2026. You can guess from the "CAP Knowledge" title of the challenge that it's about CAP, and specifically knowledge of CAP. What does that mean, and perhaps more interestingly, why?

(Alternatively, jump straight to the questions.)

Background

Both questions can be answered together, with one phrase: Cognitive Discipline.

Today's AI facilities in the area of development are as wonderful as they are powerful. There's no denying their ability to help us level up with regards to building and developing, not least in the SAP world.

While we continue to build, today's era and AI context means it's actually more important than ever to retain ... no - scratch that ... to double down on our understanding, our knowledge of the tools, libraries, architectures, frameworks and best practices that we and our agentic colleagues are using.

There's a significant possibility that we will start to lose the ability to reason about what's being built, to understand, validate and review the architecture approaches, the library design and use, and the code - whether that's declarative or procedural or something entirely different.

That in turn leads to largely as yet unknown challenges down the line, not only challenges for the maintainability of what's been built, but our ability to retain enough knowledge to continue to steer its direction and ensure the best possible outcomes.

Ironically, the best approach to mitigating this risk, is to turn to what (in these days of search-first and AI summaries of specific issues or solutions) is becoming a little out of fashion ... and that is reading.

Reading + thinking = comprehension.

And that is the simple key to practising and retaining what some folks today are referring to as "cognitive discipline", the active and conscious effort to remain relevant, retain knowledge of the tech we're steeped within, and grow our ability to continually vet and monitor the design and implementation of the apps and services that are being created.

The challenge

This Developer Challenge is in that context, in that it is designed to:

  • help us attain and then retain our understanding of CAP
  • celebrate the accomplished and exemplary documentation unofficially and affectionately known as "Capire"

And we all know what "Capire" means, right? :=)

So this month's challenge is a series of questions, each of which has a simple and concise answer. The questions are deliberately based on content in Capire, and each question's intro will hint at where in the content you should explore to find the answer.

And that's the key to this challenge, and (hopefully also) the fun and the beauty. The questions have been designed to cause you to dive into Capire, in search of the answers. But don't rush! When you get there, take your time, spend some extra minutes exploring and getting acquainted with what's there, how the information is structured and presented, what the navigation feels like, and perhaps even try out the realtime search (hint: use Ctrl-K or Cmd-K).

It's about the journey.

Taking part

We're using the approach we first introduced back in 2023 for the August challenge on APIs. So the idea is that each answer will be a single word, or technical term, usually without spaces. Note also that the answer values are case sensitive, so ensure you get that right too!

And to submit your answer, you should use the same mechanism as we used for that Aug 2023 challenge, namely the "answer obfuscator", or "value hasher", described in the Task 0 post of that challenge back then: SAP Developer Challenge - APIs - Task 0 - Learn to share your task results.

Before embarking on this challenge, please head over there to that Task 0 post to make sure you "understand the assignment", which is basically for each question:

  • read the question details, picking up hints on where to look in Capire
  • determine your answer
  • use the hashing mechanism to hash your answer
  • submit the resulting hash (and nothing else!) in a reply to the individual question thread

This is simply so that we can all have a go at answering the questions, and submitting our answers, without spoiling it (revealing the answer) for everyone else. And, if I can conjure up the right incantations, I can automate the checking of the answers too 🙂

The questions

We'll post a new thread, with a new question, every Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday of this month (June). So be sure to keep an eye on the new posts here. To help you find them, we'll also add a link to each new question post to this post (below), as they're created.

  • 01 (Mon 01 Jun) Common types and aspects
  • 02 (Tue 02 Jun) Content for common reuse types
  • 03 (Wed 03 Jun) Declarative field control with @mandatory

entry.title: SAP Developer News, May 28th, 2026
entry.content:
Items

CodeJam report live from Mexico with Tom and Rich
- SAP CodeJam topics https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407

Building an LLM Agent using CAP
- Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/building-an-llm-agent-using-cap/ba-p/14404223

Sapphire 2026 highlights for SAP Business Data Cloud
- SAP Business Data Cloud: Top New Features | SAP Sapphire 2026 Highlights https://youtu.be/cOnmdvZk5xo
- “Mastering SAP Data Architecture” new course https://learning.sap.com/courses/mastering-sap-data-architecture

Developer challenges May into June
- Challenge overview (May) https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/may-2026-developer-challenge-from-events-to-intelligence-building-an-event/ba-p/14386772
- Challenge overview (June) https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-cap-blog-posts/june-developer-challenge-cap-knowledge/ba-p/14405157 

Did You Know? #18 – Sharing and synchronising profiles
- ABAP Cleaner https://github.com/SAP/abap-cleaner

Chapter titles

00:00 Intro
00:07 CodeJam report live from Mexico with Tom and Rich
02:00 Building an LLM Agent using CAP
03:20 Sapphire 2026 highlights for SAP Business Data Cloud
05:21 Developer Challenges May into June
07:00 DYK #18 Sharing and synchronising profiles

Transcript

[Tom & Rich]
Hey, everybody. We're coming to you from a Code Jam Roadshow in Mexico. We started the week in Mexico City, Mexico. Rich, you had the first day. What were you teaching? Yeah, so we did ABAP Cloud and Joule, and we had a great day. We went through the exercises and saw how Joule can assist us in creating RAP applications quickly and easily. and everybody seemed to have a good time yeah and then I led day two which was the code-based agents code jam where we were looking at creating agents from scratch also how agents help us with coding activities we gave a little look at the new Joule Studio but also solved a crime who stole our painting from the gallery using multiple agents working together. So we get to see how different personas can be applied to agents, how different levels of knowledge and expertise can be applied to agents and how then they work together in a crew to solve a problem. Fun stuff. Yeah. And here we are Wednesday. It was a travel day to Monterey. I think that's where we are. That's all right. You see the beautiful mountains behind us. outside the city. And the moon, right? Yeah, and a crane, evidently. But now we're going to do it again. So all over again tomorrow, we're doing another code jam here in Monterey. Is it at the SAP offices? Tomorrow's at the SAP offices. Again, we're doing all ABAP cloud and Joule. Some of our friends are coming from Mexico City as well. And yeah, so we'll have a good time again. All right. Yeah. So until next time, maybe we'll be coming to a city near you in the near future check out all the new stuff that we have yep bye for now.

[Ajay]
Hello everyone agentic systems have been gaining significant traction lately and justifiably so they enable the development of ai systems capable of iteratively reasoning and executing toward the goal-oriented solutions while numerous frameworks and libraries exist for building standalone agents The SAP Cloud Application Programming Model now presents a distinct opportunity to architect agents that encapsulate domain -specific knowledge and seamlessly integrate with SAP ecosystem, including its native services and backend systems. We have a tutorial blog post by Johannes Vogt from CAP Enablement Team, where he walks us through the implementation of a lightweight LLM -powered agent leveraging the CAP framework. CAP integrates nicely with identity workflow and allows us to easily expose our services as tools for the agent, while also ensuring that all the necessary service level logic and permissions are applied. Since production-like qualities like authentication, authorization, and database transactions are handled by CAP, we can focus on the domain. There are further links to the blog post that you can explore for advanced topic and use cases. So give it a try today and begin your agentic journey using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Links to the blog post will be in the description below.

[Witalij]
If you are interested in all things data but missed Sapphire, or you just want the short version without sitting through hours and hours of sessions, our product expert at SAP, KP Sauer, has you covered. He recorded a few-minute recap of top new features in SAP Business Data Cloud to help you quickly catch up on the most important announcements. Here are some of the key topics he covered in his video. The announcement of the SAP Business AI Platform and what actually changed from an architectural perspective. Why SAP HANA Cloud is becoming a core component of the Business Data Cloud strategy. How Reltio and SAP Master Data Governance fit together inside BDC. And finally, Joule agents operating directly within the business data fabric. So, if you want the high -level takeaways without the noise, this is definitely You'll find the link in the description. Plus, this week, the Mastering SAP Data Architecture course officially went live on learning.sap.com, alongside the launch of the new SAP Certified Data Architect Certification. This is not a generic introduction
course. It is designed for data practitioners and data architects who need to operate on architectural depth and understand how enterprise data strategy is evolving in the AI era. The course covers topics like architecting data strategy for the agentic AI era, applying established frameworks including TOGAF, DAMA DMBOK and the SAP enterprise architecture framework in real-world scenarios and exploring concepts like data mesh, semantic layers, and knowledge graphs for enterprise AI. So if you are working in the enterprise data architecture, analytics, governance, or AI strategy, this is definitely worth your time as well.

[DJ]
The developer challenge for the month of May is coming to an end this week. Antonio did a great job accompanying and guiding us on a journey to learn about event driven architectures and AI. In week one, we acquainted ourselves with events. In week two, we connected to the broker and started to consume those events. In week three, we looked at vectorizing the event payload. And in this final week, week four, we're looking at storing vectors and enabling retrieval augmented generation. That's RAG. While this month's challenge is still active, June is just around the corner. So we wanted to give you a heads up that the next challenge is ready and will start on Monday. June's challenge encourages you to dig into the wonders and delights of the CAP documentation, aka CAPIRE, to unearth truth and beauty in this exemplary and valuable resource. Why? Because today, more than ever, we as developers need to ensure we retain knowledge and double down on our understanding of architectures, toolkits, frameworks, and perhaps most of all, of best practices. More on that in the challenge intro post, which is published now. When the challenge starts, you can also head back to this challenge intro post to find your way to the tasks. Good luck, have fun, and as always, happy learning.

[Sheena]
Did you know that you can maintain a synchronized ABAP profile across your team by creating and sharing a team profile in ABAP Cleaner? Usually, ABAP Cleaner profiles are created locally based on the required rules. Let us consider a scenario where a project team would like to come up with a synchronized profile that can be used by everyone in the team. Here, the lead developer or architect can formulate a profile where all the required rules are activated and this profile is then exported using the export button to a synchronized folder, which may be the team's OneDrive or SharePoint. Team members can then use this profile by clicking on the folders button to add the synchronized folder as an additional read-only source of team profiles. In case of any further changes, we can simply export it again to override the synchronized folder and thereby sharing the updates to everyone, provided it's not the default profile.

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content:
CDS provides a set of common types and aspects for reuse in CDS models, so we can avoid reinventing the wheel each time. Moreover, we end up with concise models that are easier to understand, and our models are more interoperable.

In this set, there's the managed aspect which adds four elements to an entity, and each of those four elements will be automatically filled with data on write-semantic operations on the entity. The definition of this aspect uses annotations to determine the events on which the data is written.

According to the Capire documentation, what is the annotation that is used in the definition of elements in this managed aspect, the annotation which will cause data to be written on UPDATE and UPSERT operations (specify just the @-based name of the annotation, not the entire @NAME: value construct).

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

Further info:

  • The Aspect managed section of the Common Types and Aspects topic describes the managed aspect.
  • The Managed Data section of the Domain Modeling topic goes in to more detail on the annotations.
  • Best practice BES006 Compose with reuse in mind.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content: (Please ignore the hyperlinked and uppercased "NAME" in the question text, it is the platform's automatic linking of @ prefixed names to members, read it as an @ symbol followed by 'name' in lower case)

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content: @SachinArtani it won't even have a chance at working as you've included extra content in the reply 🙂 Please see the Taking part section for more info.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content: @SachinArtani your response still contains extra content - for example, the text "I hope this works -" and the formatting of the hash. See other example responses here for what to do 👍

entry.title: Re: CodeJam Request Freiburg Germany
entry.content: Hi @jakobgriebenow - I'm on Tom's team and would be very happy to come to your location and run the CodeJam. I'll contact you directly for further coordination and planning. Cheers. DJ

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
entry.content:
A set of common types and aspects is great, and our models are the better for using them. But what about actual data? There are some types, which are defined as associations to entities, and it would be useful to be able to have access to and load initial data for these entities. The types concerned are Country, Currency, Language and Timezone.

There is actually an NPM package available in the @SAPnamespace that contains such initial data.

What is the name (including the namespace) of this package?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

Further info:

  • The Using Prebuilt Content Package section of the Common Types and Aspects topic.
  • The Common Reuse Types section of the Common Types and Aspects topic details these four types.
  • The blog post ISO content for common CAP types explains more, with an example.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
entry.content: (Please ignore the hyperlinked and uppercased "SAP" in the question text, it is the platform's automatic linking of @ prefixed names to members, read it as an @ symbol followed by 'sap' in lower case)

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
entry.content:
Among the CDS annotations for declaring constraints when modelling, there are a handful that can be used to drive behaviour and restrictions in Fiori UIs, by causing the generation of appropriate annotations in the service's metadata.

One of these annotations is @mandatory, and this causes an OData annotation to be generated on the target that corresponds to the element annotated in CDS:

<Annotations Target="...">
  <Annotation Term="..." EnumMember="..." />
</Annotations>

The OData vocabulary Common contains the term FieldControl. In the <Annotation ... /> element that is generated, there's an attribute Term which points to this term, and a second attribute EnumMember which contains the fully qualified value for that term, a value which represents "mandatory".

What is that fully qualified value?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
entry.content: Thanks @HarshPatelV ! I agree, I always like to understand what's going on under the surface. That way I can appreciate even more the calmness _above_ the surface 🙂

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
entry.content: @TerryClose17 see the link in the description above, specifically the one to 'Taking part' in the part in italics. Or jump directly to the hashing mechanism.
